rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Granting Leave To File A Complaint-In-Intervention Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 387
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Proposed intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co.'s motion for an order granting leave to file a complaint-in-intervention pursuant to C.C.P. § 387 is granted. Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. have leave to file a complaint-in-intervention. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2018 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.'s motion to dismiss action is continued to May 16, 2018 pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(B). Defendant failed to lodge with the Court a courtesy copy of its moving papers and it must lodge one no later than May 7, 2018. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2018 in Department 503, Line 7. Defendant Colgate-Palmolive Company's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant's special interrogatories and plaintiff's responses thereto demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Defendant's Exhibits 36, 37.) Plaintiff failed to present evidence sufficient to create a triable issue as to whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiff's evidence is sufficient to demonstrate that defendant knew of the hazards of asbestos, before or at least during plaintiff's exposure to Cashmere Bouquet, plaintiff's evidence is not sufficient to create a triable issue as to whether defendant knew that its talc supplies and/or Cashmere Bouquet were contaminated with asbestos. Nor did plaintiff's evidence create a triable issue whether defendant had reason to know that its talc supplies and/or Cashmere Bouquet had the potential to be contaminated with asbestos at a time prior to or during plaintiff's use of the product. Rather, plaintiff's evidence demonstrates that once defendant learned of the potential for asbestos contamination in talc, it took steps to address the issue. This evidence does not rise to the level of being despicable conduct carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (Plaintiff's Exhibits G, I, J, K, L, M, N, P, U, V, W, AA.) While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objection to the declaration of Sean Fitzgerald is sustained. (Hirano v. Hirano (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 1; Perry v. Hawthorne (2017) 2 Cal.5th 536.) The Court did not consider Mr. Fitzgerald's declaration or any of the exhibits attached thereto. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)
